nav.title: ONLINE SMITH CHART TOOL
nav.urlCopied: Shareable URL copied to clipboard! Your whole website state is in the URL

app.intro: Smith charts can help you design matching networks and obtain maximum power transfer between your source and load. Plot s-parameters.
app.urlLoadedSnackbar: Some settings were loaded from the URL. Please click here to reset to the default state.
app.plotImpedanceS1p: Plot Reflection Coefficient Looking Into DP1
app.supportLink: Get professional support from Microwave Master here

graph.stabilityCirclesToast: If no circles appear it is because the entire Smith Chart is stable. More details in Stability Tutorial
graph.idealTooltip: set all ESR and ESL to zero
graph.hoverOutside: Move cursor back inside the circle

custom.desc1: Enter rows of impedance vs increasing frequency
custom.desc2: Don't enter units or characters; use 2440e6 notation for 2440MHz
custom.helperText: If the textbox contains a comma it's assumed your data is comma separated, otherwise assumes whitespace separated. Each line must have 3 non-blank numberical values. The only accepted characters are 0-9, '-', '+', '.', e, E and ','. Frequency must be increasing

sparam.tableIntro: ABOVE TEXT FORMATTED INTO A TABLE
sparam.noiseTitle: Noise Data - note that noise frequencies not in s-param are discarded

impedance.enterZHelper: Enter numeric impedance (will be passed to the component calculator)

labels.eeffHelper: Note - physical line length is changed

circuit.shortedStubDialog: Shorted-stub arc starts at 0-ohms (length = 0), therefore will not be connected to the previous point. This may seem unintuitive but makes more sense once considering frequency-span and tolerance
circuit.hint: Click components above to add them to the circuit below. Impedance is looking {{direction}}

tutorials.title: Tutorials
tutorials.noise: Designing for optimimum Noise Figure & Gain

release.title: Release Notes
v21: Added gain circles for turning s2p gain
Added a couple of s-parameter tutorials
release.v20a: Site moved from will-kelsey.com/smith_chart/ to onlinesmithchart.com
release.v20b: Whole site re-written. Over 6 years many features were added to the old site which left the code very messy. Some users were looking at the code to verify implementations, which was tough. Now, the code is much more organised and optimized. In the future I hope the community can add features and improvements to the codebase.
release.v20c: Moved from vanilla JS to React + MUI. This allows: running lint, more maintainable code, smaller file size, many micro-benefits from joining the mainstream
release.v20d: As well as a re-write, the following new features are added:
v20features: Smith chart is interactive - hover over the chart. This makes it possible to see Z when there are N curves (tol, fspan)
Components have sliders - quickly see whether to increase or decrease component values
Save whole state in the URL
release.v10italic: Adding a brief history of the site
release.v10a: I needed to match a Maxim 2.4GHz bluetooth amplifier (with a 25+25j output impedace) to a 50ohm chip antenna. The only software I could find was from Fritz Dellsperger however on Windows 11 the GUI became unusable.
release.v10b: The first version of this site was extremely simple to support my basic needs; a black box, capacitor, inductor and smith chart diagram. We successfully used the tool to chose our component values.
release.v10c: The community has made hundreds of requests over the years and many features have been added
release.v10d: All features have been verified against Fritz's software, YouTube videos, allaboutcircuits.com, etc. Of coursethere were some mistakes, most of these have been identified and fixed during the 100's of comments

errors.sparamUrlTooLong: The saved state included a 1000+ character s-parameter file. That was not saved so must be manually re-entered

cal.realisticDesc: Enter residual model parameters for each standard (frequency-independent polynomial coefficients)
cal.planeDPDesc: Selecting DPn shows the reflection seen at that point, removing the effect of outer components

pe.desc: Port extension shifts the reference plane by adding an electrical delay. The original (pre-extension) trace is shown as a dashed overlay on the Smith chart.

de.deembedDesc: De-embedding removes the fixture S-parameters from the measured data using T-matrix cascade.
de.embedDesc: Embedding adds the fixture S-parameters to the DUT data.
de.sparamHint: Upload a .s2p Touchstone file as the fixture model. Asymmetric fixtures: use separate files for port 1 and port 2.

tdr.resolutionInfo: Resolution: {{res_ns}} ns ({{res_mm}} mm) | Max time span: {{span_ns}} ns
tdr.noData: No S-parameter data loaded. Add an S-parameter component to see the time-domain transform.
tdr.gateApplied: Gate applied — gated trace (green dashed) is shown on the Smith chart alongside the original.
tdr.gateHint: Editing any of Start / Stop / Center / Span automatically updates the others. The gate region is shaded on the time-domain plot.

unc.desc: Uncertainty bands (shaded) are shown on all S11 magnitude plots and as ellipses around frequency points on the Smith chart. Dominant error source and worst-case uncertainty are summarised below each plot.

pipeline.splitChartDesc: When on, the corrected data is shown on a dedicated second Smith chart. When off, corrections are overlaid on the main chart.
pipeline.orderNote: Pipeline order: Calibration → De-embedding → Port Extension → Gating
